summary: Of the 10 benchmark problems, 7 (q1, q2, q3, q7, q8, q9, q10) appear fully resolved with single proof issues closed, while q4, q5, and q6 carry the bulk of ongoing research activity. q6 is closest to completion with only 1 open issue against 15 resolved, whereas q4 (22 open) and q5 (14 open) remain the most contested and require sustained attention.
problems: q4 has the highest open-issue count (22 open vs 23 resolved), including a limiting argument from simple to general roots, a Lemma 1 implicit-differentiation gap, and a dimensional error (JJ* vs J*J) in the Hessian.
q5 has 14 open issues centered on structural rigor: a converse connectivity bookkeeping argument, well-definedness of chi^O(H) as a minimum, and an unsupported 'discrete slices' conclusion.
q6 has 1 lingering open issue: a missing rank argument that the top-sigma barrier holds, related to a Barrier Update Lemma with implicit rank assumptions.
Recurring pattern across open issues: gaps in rank/dimensional bookkeeping, unjustified limiting/generalization steps, and well-definedness or implicit-assumption concerns.
highlights: 7 of 10 problems (q1, q2, q3, q7, q8, q9, q10) are fully resolved with their single proof issue closed.
q6 is on the verge of resolution with a 15:1 resolved-to-open ratio, needing only the top-sigma barrier rank argument.
q4 shows strong traction with 23 issues already resolved, indicating active and productive engagement despite high remaining count.
q5 has more resolved than typical contested problems start with (10 resolved), suggesting steady forward progress.

suggested_todos.title: Close the single open rank argument on q6 (top-sigma barrier) to finalize the proof
Resolve q4's dimensional error (JJ* vs J*J in Hessian) and validate the simple-to-general-roots limiting argument
Address q5 well-definedness of chi^O(H) and shore up the 'discrete slices' conclusion
Audit q4 and q5 open issues for the recurring rank/dimensional bookkeeping pattern and apply fixes systematically
Spot-check the 7 resolved problems for any overlooked dependencies before declaring round complete
